findings.claim: A subpopulation of vertically oriented bipolar cortical cells co-expressing calretinin and VIP fire bursts of action potentials at an irregular frequency, defining the irregular-spiking firing class.
findings.claim_source_sentence: (3) Finally, a small population of vertically oriented bipolar cells, termed irregular spiking cells (n = 15), fired bursts of action potentials at an irregular frequency.
findings.effect_size: n = 15 irregular spiking cells (out of 97 nonpyramidal cells); they consistently co-expressed calretinin and VIP
findings.evidence: whole-cell patch-clamp + single-cell RT-PCR, sensory-motor cortex slices, n=97 nonpyramidal cells of which 15 were irregular spiking
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: They consistently co-expressed calretinin and vasoactive intestinal polypeptide.
findings.study_system: rat sensory-motor cortex acute slices, whole-cell + scRT-PCR

findings.claim: The irregular spiking firing pattern of bipolar VIPergic neocortical interneurons is mediated by an I_D-like (Kv1-family) K+ current that can be neutralised by 4-AP, dendrotoxin-I or dendrotoxin-K to convert the cell to sustained firing.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Several seconds of depolarization, micromolar concentrations of 4-aminopyridine, and nanomolar concentrations of either dendrotoxin I or K converted this irregular pattern to a sustained discharge, suggesting the involvement of an ID-like K+ current.
findings.effect_size: qualitative - irregular pattern converted to sustained discharge by 4-AP and dendrotoxin
findings.evidence: rat neocortex slices P16-22, whole-cell + scRT-PCR + paired recordings + pharmacology (4-AP, dendrotoxin)
findings.study_system: rat neocortex acute slices (P16-22)

findings.claim: Unsupervised clustering of fusiform neocortical interneurons (n=60) identified three discrete physio-molecular groups, the third (n=16) being dominated by irregular-spiking VIPergic cells, supporting VIP-IS as a separable e-type.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Finally, the last type of fusiform neuron (n = 16) contained a majority of irregular spiking-VIPergic neurons.
findings.effect_size: 3 distinct clusters; cluster sizes n=12 (RSNP-SS), n=32 (RSNP-VIP), n=16 (IS-VIP)
findings.evidence: rat neocortex slices, patch-clamp + multiplex scRT-PCR, hierarchical clustering on multiple electrophysiological + molecular parameters
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: The second type of fusiform neuron (n = 32), termed RSNP-VIP cluster, predominantly expressed VIP and also showed firing properties of RSNP neurons with accommodation profiles different from those of RSNP-SS cells.
findings.study_system: rat neocortex acute slices, n=60 fusiform interneurons

findings.claim: Nicotinic receptor activation selectively depolarizes neocortical VIP/CCK-coexpressing interneurons while having no effect on pyramidal cells, PV fast-spiking, or SST interneurons, identifying a cell-type-specific intrinsic-excitability response distinguishing VIP cells from PV/SST.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Nicotinic receptor agonists had no effect on pyramidal neurons and on most types of interneurons, including parvalbumin-expressing fast spiking interneurons and somatostatin-expressing interneurons, but selectively excited a subpopulation of interneurons coexpressing the neuropeptides vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP) and cholecystokinin.
findings.effect_size: Selective excitation in VIP+CCK+ interneurons; null effect in pyramidal/PV/SST
findings.evidence: rat neocortical slices, whole-cell + scRT-PCR, pharmacology (nicotine, DHβE, mecamylamine, MLA, TTX, low Ca2+)
findings.study_system: rat neocortex acute slices

findings.claim: In mouse entorhinal cortex, hierarchical clustering of intrinsic properties yields physiologically defined cell types that align with molecular identity (VIP, SST, 5HT3a-R, NPY-NGF, NPY-non-NGF, RCAN2), and five intrinsic features predict molecular identity with 81.4% accuracy.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Finally, we reduced the complex multidimensional space of intrinsic properties to the most salient five that predicted the cellular biomolecular identity with 81.4% accuracy.
findings.effect_size: 81.4% prediction accuracy from 5 intrinsic features
findings.evidence: mouse EC acute slices; whole-cell recordings of stellate, pyramidal, and 6 molecularly defined interneuron classes; hierarchical clustering
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Finally, we reduced the complex multidimensional space of intrinsic properties to the most salient five that predicted the cellular biomolecular identity with 81.4% accuracy.
findings.study_system: mouse entorhinal cortex acute slices

findings.claim: The irregular-spiking (IS) firing pattern of cortical VIP interneurons depends on KCNQ-mediated M-current — pharmacological block (linopirdine) converts IS VIP-INs to tonic firing while leaving continuous-adapting (CA) VIP-INs unchanged, identifying KCNQ5/M-current as the molecular substrate of the IS phenotype.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Blockade of M-current with the KCNQ channel inhibitor linopirdine converted IS VIP-INs to a tonic firing mode but had no effect on CA VIP-INs.
findings.effect_size: qualitative: linopirdine converts IS to tonic in IS VIP-INs; null effect in CA VIP-INs
findings.evidence: mouse barrel cortex slices, whole-cell patch from VIP-Cre x Ai14 mice; pharmacology (linopirdine 10 μM); current-clamp characterization of IS vs CA VIP-INs
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Blockade of M-current with the KCNQ channel inhibitor linopirdine converted IS VIP-INs to a tonic firing mode but had no effect on CA VIP-INs.
findings.study_system: mouse barrel cortex acute slices, VIP-Cre

findings.claim: In adult mouse CA1, hippocampal type-3 interneuron-specific (I-S3) VIP+/CR+ cells have very high input resistance (~500 MΩ) and a slow membrane time constant (~36 ms), values that are unchanged in VIP-Tg amyloid-overexpressing animals despite firing-rate deficits.

findings.effect_size: VIP-nonTg I-S3: Vrest −60.5 ± 1.7 mV, Rin 517 ± 37 MΩ, Cm 32.8 ± 3.5 pF, τm 36.5 ± 3.2 ms (n=7 cells/5 animals)
findings.evidence: targeted whole-cell patch-clamp from VIP-eGFP/VIP-Tg+ I-S3 cells in adult mouse CA1 acute slices
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Notably, the resting membrane potential, input resistance, membrane capacitance, and membrane time constant of I-S3 cells showed no significant differences between VIP-nonTg (−60.5 ± 1.7 mV, 517 ± 37 MΩ, 32.8 ± 3.5 pF, 36.5 ± 3.2 ms) and VIP-Tg mice (−65.8 ± 2.1 mV, 493 ± 45 MΩ, 29.3 ± 2.9 pF, 35.7
findings.study_system: mouse hippocampus CA1, VIP-eGFP, acute slices, adult

findings.claim: In mouse basolateral amygdala, VIP+ irregular-spiking interneurons have ~70% higher input resistance than CCK+/CB1+ basket cells (350 ± 28 MΩ vs 203 ± 28 MΩ, p<0.001) and lower rheobase (43 ± 5 pA vs 133 ± 17 pA), with no difference in AP threshold, identifying high R_in / low rheobase as a robust feature of VIP-IS cells.

findings.study_system: mouse BLA acute slices, VIP-IRES-cre × Ai14

findings.claim: Cortical CR-expressing GABAergic interneurons partition into two developmentally distinct populations — CGE-derived VIP+ bipolar cells and dorsal-MGE-derived SOM+ Martinotti-like cells — implying that VIP/CR co-expressing 'bipolar' cells are a CGE-specific cohort with their own intrinsic-electrophysiological signature.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Cortical GABAergic CR+ interneurons can be divided in two main populations: VIP-bipolar interneurons deriving from the CGE and SOM-Martinotti-like interneurons originating in the dorsal MGE.
findings.effect_size: qualitative — two CR+ populations distinguishable by developmental origin and ephys
findings.evidence: review of morphology, molecular markers, and intrinsic electrophysiology of cortical CR+ interneurons across rodent neocortex
findings.study_system: rodent neocortex (review)

findings.claim: Patch-seq enables simultaneous extraction of intrinsic electrophysiology, morphology and full transcriptome from single cortical interneurons; in 67 L1 interneurons the joint Patch-seq profile shows strong agreement between firing-pattern-based class (eNGC vs SBC) and transcriptomic class.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In a separate set of experiments, we carried out patch-clamping on an additional set of 67 L1 interneurons in acute cortical slices using the Patch-seq protocol.
findings.effect_size: n=67 L1 interneurons profiled with all three modalities; concordance between firing-pattern class and transcriptomic class qualitatively strong
findings.evidence: rat juvenile somatosensory cortex acute slices; Patch-seq workflow combining whole-cell recordings, morphology recovery, and scRNA-seq; n=67 L1 interneurons
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: In a separate set of experiments, we carried out patch-clamping on an additional set of 67 L1 interneurons in acute cortical slices using the Patch-seq protocol.
findings.study_system: rat L1 somatosensory cortex acute slices, Patch-seq

findings.claim: Patch-seq combines whole-cell patch-clamp electrophysiology with full-soma RNA-seq from individual cortical neurons and resolves transcriptomic subtypes that align with intrinsic electrophysiological properties, supporting transcriptome-electrophysiology mapping at single-cell resolution.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We show that Patch-seq reveals a close link between electrophysiological characteristics, responses to acute chemical challenges and RNA expression of neurotransmitter receptors and channels.
findings.effect_size: qualitative — close link between intrinsic ephys characteristics and transcriptome
findings.evidence: mouse brain slices, somatic-aspiration scRNA-seq after patch-clamp protocol with intrinsic ephys characterisation
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: We show that Patch-seq reveals a close link between electrophysiological characteristics, responses to acute chemical challenges and RNA expression of neurotransmitter receptors and channels.
findings.study_system: mouse cortical pyramidal cells and interneurons, acute slices

findings.claim: Patch-seq across mouse primary motor cortex shows that intrinsic-electrophysiological properties of Vip transcriptomic types vary continuously rather than discretely along the transcriptomic axis — e.g., membrane time constant peaks at the Sncg-adjacent end and decreases toward Vip-Gpc3 — supporting a graded morpho-electric landscape within the VIP subclass.
findings.claim_source_sentence: For example, electrophysiological properties of the t-types within the Vip subclass varied continuously across the transcriptomic landscape; the membrane time constant, for instance, had its largest values close to the Sncg subclass and gradually decreased towards Vip Gpc3
findings.effect_size: qualitative — continuous gradient of τm across Vip t-types; ranked highest near Sncg, lowest at Vip Gpc3
findings.evidence: adult mouse MOp acute slices, Patch-seq (whole-cell intrinsic ephys + Smart-seq2 + morphology) across all layers and Cre lines
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: For example, electrophysiological properties of the t-types within the Vip subclass varied continuously across the transcriptomic landscape; the membrane time constant, for instance, had its largest values close to the Sncg subclass and gradually decreased towards Vip Gpc3
findings.study_system: adult mouse MOp Patch-seq, ~P75

findings.claim: Human neocortical Patch-seq (n=675 across LAMP5/PAX6=75, VIP=99, SST=149, PVALB=352) demonstrates that the four GABAergic subclasses are separable in UMAP of intrinsic electrophysiological features, with VIP cells displaying diverse morphologies (bipolar dendrites, descending axon) and intrinsic firing ranging from adapting to irregular spiking — consistent with rodent ME-types.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Comprehensive analysis of the associated voltage traces for the four GABAergic subclasses, LAMP5/PAX6 ( n = 75), VIP ( n = 99), SST ( n = 149), and PVALB ( n = 352), revealed distinct passive and active electrophysiological features
findings.effect_size: n: LAMP5/PAX6=75, VIP=99, SST=149, PVALB=352; subclasses separable in UMAP of intrinsic ephys features
findings.evidence: Patch-seq from human neurosurgical neocortical tissue (n=675 GABAergic neurons), with morphological reconstruction and transcriptomic mapping
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Comprehensive analysis of the associated voltage traces for the four GABAergic subclasses, LAMP5/PAX6 ( n = 75), VIP ( n = 99), SST ( n = 149), and PVALB ( n = 352), revealed distinct passive and active electrophysiological features
findings.study_system: human neocortex (live surgical tissue) Patch-seq

findings.claim: Cross-species comparative motor cortex taxonomy (BICCN) shows that Vip GABAergic subtypes are markedly more diversified between humans/marmosets and mice than between primates, indicating species-specific expansion of the VIP transcriptomic landscape that frames cross-species comparisons of VIP intrinsic electrophysiology.
findings.claim_source_sentence: More consensus clusters could be resolved by pairwise alignment between humans and marmosets than between either of these primates and mice, particularly for Vip subtypes
findings.effect_size: qualitative — more Vip consensus types preserved between human/marmoset than human/mouse
findings.evidence: multi-omic atlas (snRNA-seq, snATAC-seq, snmC-seq2) of M1 across human, marmoset, mouse; multi-modal alignment of consensus clusters
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: More consensus clusters could be resolved by pairwise alignment between humans and marmosets than between either of these primates and mice, particularly for Vip subtypes
findings.study_system: cross-species M1 multi-modal atlas (human, marmoset, mouse)

findings.claim: In contrast to mouse, where Vip+ L1 interneurons localize to deeper L1, the equivalent VIP transcriptomic types in human cortex spread across L1, indicating species divergence in laminar position of L1-VIP cells that constrains cross-species comparisons of L1 VIP intrinsic-ephys recordings.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Similarly, given the sublaminar selectivity of thalamic projections to Vip + versus Ndnf + cells in mouse L1 ( 56 ), the human L1 VIP types which are located in deeper layers in mouse may be especially relevant to thalamocortical microcircuit structure.
findings.effect_size: qualitative — VIP L1 types occupy deeper L1 in mouse, broader L1 distribution in human
findings.evidence: Patch-seq atlas of L1 GABAergic interneurons in human (n=323) and mouse (n=440) cortex, with cross-species transcriptomic alignment and morphology
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Similarly, given the sublaminar selectivity of thalamic projections to Vip + versus Ndnf + cells in mouse L1 ( 56 ), the human L1 VIP types which are located in deeper layers in mouse may be especially relevant to thalamocortical microcircuit structure.
findings.study_system: human and mouse cortical L1 Patch-seq cross-species

findings.claim: VIP-expressing GABAergic interneurons constitute ~12% of neocortical interneurons and are concentrated in superficial layers; they preferentially target SST interneurons to disinhibit cortical principal cells, providing the cell-type framework against which VIP intrinsic-ephys properties (high R_in, IS/CA firing) are compared.
findings.claim_source_sentence: A third major group of INs is distinguished by the expression of the neuropeptide vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP); these interneurons account for ~12% of the INs in the neocortex and include cells that preferentially target other interneurons, particularly SST INs, and thus act to disinhibit the
findings.effect_size: VIP ~12% of cortical INs; superficial layer enrichment; preferential SST targeting
findings.evidence: review and primary data from Id2-Cre cortical Patch-seq mouse studies
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: A third major group of INs is distinguished by the expression of the neuropeptide vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP); these interneurons account for ~12% of the INs in the neocortex and include cells that preferentially target other interneurons, particularly SST INs, and thus act to disinhibit the
findings.study_system: mouse neocortex (review + Patch-seq)

findings.claim: A Patch-seq-trained classifier of human cortical interneuron subclass identity (PVALB vs non-PVALB) is dominated by four intrinsic features — AP height, depolarizing sag, AP upstroke adaptation ratio, and membrane time constant — providing a quantitative basis for distinguishing VIP/LAMP5/SST cells from PV cells using intrinsic ephys alone.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The features with highest weighting in the classifier were AP height, depolarizing sag, AP upstroke adaptation ratio, and membrane time constant ( Supplementary file 2 ).
findings.effect_size: top-4 weighted features: AP height, sag, AP upstroke adaptation ratio, τm
findings.evidence: human cortical Patch-seq from AAV-labeled slice cultures; intrinsic-feature classifier trained against single-cell Patch-seq reference
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: The features with highest weighting in the classifier were AP height, depolarizing sag, AP upstroke adaptation ratio, and membrane time constant ( Supplementary file 2 ).
findings.study_system: human cortical slice cultures + Patch-seq

findings.claim: In CA1 hippocampal VIP interneurons selectively responsive to nicotinic ACh, the resting membrane potential is −73.9 ± 0.8 mV and input resistance is 395.3 ± 20.8 MΩ (n=30), with subpopulations differing in sag (sag ratio 1.09 ± 0.01) and firing pattern (irregular vs accommodating regular).
findings.claim_source_sentence: The average resting membrane potential for nicotinic responding VIP interneurons was −73.9 mV ± 0.8 mV ( n = 30) and the average input resistance was 395.3 MΩ, ± 20.8 MΩ ( n = 30).
findings.effect_size: Vrest = −73.9 ± 0.8 mV; R_in = 395.3 ± 20.8 MΩ; sag ratio 1.09 ± 0.01; accommodation ratio 2.75 ± 0.84 (n=30)
findings.evidence: VIP-Cre × YFP mouse hippocampal CA1 acute slices, whole-cell patch-clamp from 30 nicotinic-responding VIP cells
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: The average resting membrane potential for nicotinic responding VIP interneurons was −73.9 mV ± 0.8 mV ( n = 30) and the average input resistance was 395.3 MΩ, ± 20.8 MΩ ( n = 30).
findings.study_system: mouse hippocampus CA1, VIP-Cre × YFP, acute slices

findings.claim: In the inferior colliculus, VIP-Cre-labelled cells are glutamatergic stellate neurons (~20% of ICc stellate cells) with sustained, near-linear firing — 90.3% sustained, 8.4% adapting, 1.3% other (n=237) — and minimal voltage sag, distinct from cortical GABAergic VIP irregular-spiking interneurons; CAUTION: not the cortical VIP+ GABAergic class.
findings.claim_source_sentence: VIP neurons had a resting membrane potential of −69.5 mV ± 4.4 mV (n = 216, corrected for liquid junction potential).
findings.effect_size: Vrest = −69.5 ± 4.4 mV (n=216); 90.3% sustained, 8.4% adapting (SFA ratio ≥2), 1.3% other (n=237); minimal sag
findings.evidence: VIP-IRES-Cre × Ai14 mouse, IC acute slices, whole-cell patch from 216–237 VIP+ neurons, current-step protocols
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: VIP neurons had a resting membrane potential of −69.5 mV ± 4.4 mV (n = 216, corrected for liquid junction potential).
findings.study_system: mouse inferior colliculus VIP-IRES-Cre acute slices

findings.claim: In medial PFC L1b, VIP+ interneurons display bitufted or bipolar morphology and a heterogeneous mix of non-fast-spiking (53%), irregular-spiking (29%), and fast-adapting (18%) firing patterns (n=17 cells, 8 mice), with thalamic MD inputs preferentially driving VIP+ cells over NDNF+ cells through higher intrinsic excitability.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We found VIP+ cells in L1b had bitufted or bipolar morphologies, along with non-fast-spiking (NFS), irregular-spiking (IS), or fast-adapting (fAD) firing properties (n = 17 VIP+ cells, 8 mice; NFS = 9/17, IS = 5/17, fAD = 3/17)
findings.effect_size: VIP+ L1b firing class proportions: NFS 9/17 (53%), IS 5/17 (29%), fAD 3/17 (18%)
findings.evidence: VIP-Cre × Ai14 mouse mPFC L1b acute slices, whole-cell patch from biocytin-filled VIP+ cells; circuit mapping with optogenetics from MD/VM thalamic inputs
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: We found VIP+ cells in L1b had bitufted or bipolar morphologies, along with non-fast-spiking (NFS), irregular-spiking (IS), or fast-adapting (fAD) firing properties (n = 17 VIP+ cells, 8 mice; NFS = 9/17, IS = 5/17, fAD = 3/17)
findings.study_system: mouse mPFC L1b VIP-Cre × Ai14 acute slices

findings.claim: Conditional deletion of Igf1 in VIP neurons increases inhibitory synaptic drive specifically onto VIP cells in mouse visual cortex, decreasing their firing — establishing that the experience-dependent firing rate of cortical VIP cells is set in part by VIP-cell-intrinsic IGF-1 secretion regulating their inhibitory inputs.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Given that VIP neurons disinhibit cortical circuits, it seems likely that this decreased firing of VIP neurons might alter how the cortex responds to sensory experience.
findings.effect_size: qualitative — Igf1 cKO in VIP cells increases mIPSCs onto VIP cells; no change in mEPSCs; decreased VIP firing
findings.evidence: Vip-Cre × Igf1 cKO mouse, visual cortex slices, mIPSC/mEPSC recordings; behavioural light-deprivation paradigm
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Given that VIP neurons disinhibit cortical circuits, it seems likely that this decreased firing of VIP neurons might alter how the cortex responds to sensory experience.
findings.study_system: mouse visual cortex acute slices, Vip-Cre × Igf1 cKO

findings.claim: Hippocampal/cortical VIP+ interneuron-selective type 3 (I-S3) cells exhibit a high input resistance with irregular- or regular-spiking firing patterns.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Electrophysiological characterization shows that I-S3 cells exhibit a high input resistance with irregular or regular spiking firing pattern (Chamberland et al., 2010; Tyan et al., 2014; Guet-McCreight et al., 2016).
findings.effect_size: qualitative: high input resistance with irregular/regular firing
findings.evidence: Synthesis of three independent CA1 OLM-targeting I-S3 datasets (Chamberland 2010; Tyan 2014; Guet-McCreight 2016)
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Electrophysiological characterization shows that I-S3 cells exhibit a high input resistance with irregular or regular spiking firing pattern.
findings.study_system: review of mouse CA1 I-S3 (VIP/CR) interneuron electrophysiology

findings.claim: In Scn1a+/− mouse somatosensory and visual cortex, irregular-spiking VIP+ interneurons (but not continuous-adapting VIP+ cells) show reductions in firing-rate gain, and their irregular-spiking pattern depends on M-type (KCNQ) potassium-channel activation.
findings.claim_source_sentence: These irregular spiking VIP+ cells were also found to express Nav1.1, which explains their involvement in the pathogenesis, and the irregular spiking pattern was dependent on the activation of the M-type potassium channels (Goff and Goldberg, 2019).
findings.effect_size: qualitative pharmacology: linopirdine eliminates IS firing only in IS VIP+ cells; gain reduction selective to IS VIP+ subset
findings.evidence: Goff & Goldberg 2019 (eLife 46846) showed pharmacological dependence of IS firing on KCNQ/M-current and Nav1.1 expression in IS VIP+ cells; cell-type-selective dysfunction in Scn1a haploinsufficient model.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: In addition, in Scn 1 +/− mouse somatosensory and visual cortices, VIP+ cells that displayed irregular spiking, but not those that displayed continuous adapting spiking, were found to exhibit reductions in gain (Goff and Goldberg, 2019).
findings.study_system: review summarising Goff & Goldberg 2019 (mouse S1/V1, VIP-Cre x Scn1a+/−)

findings.claim: The irregular-spiking firing pattern of cortical VIP+ neurons does not co-segregate with calretinin or CCK co-expression, nor with bipolar versus multipolar morphology — IS is therefore an electrophysiological category orthogonal to these classical anatomical/biochemical subdivisions.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Notably, the irregular spiking firing pattern did not coincide with whether VIP+ cells co-expressed CR or CCK, nor with whether the morphology was bipolar vs. multipolar.
findings.effect_size: qualitative independence between IS firing pattern and CR/CCK/morphology axes
findings.evidence: Review consolidating Pronneke 2015 (cercor/bhv202) and downstream work, which surveyed VIP morphological/firing covariation in barrel cortex.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Notably, the irregular spiking firing pattern did not coincide with whether VIP+ cells co-expressed CR or CCK, nor with whether the morphology was bipolar vs. multipolar.
findings.study_system: synthesis of mouse barrel-cortex VIP-Cre electrophysiology

findings.claim: Across all cortical layers in mouse barrel cortex VIP-Cre/tdTomato neurons, the population mean intrinsic properties are: input resistance 404.7 ± 212.8 MΩ, sag index 11.2 ± 6.8%, membrane time constant 21.4 ± 7.6 ms, rheobase 51.9 ± 33.9 pA, AP threshold −38.2 ± 3.1 mV, AP amplitude 65.1 ± 9.1 mV.
findings.claim_source_sentence: No significant differences were found in most of the basic properties such as in input resistance (population average of 404.7 ± 212.8 MΩ), sag index (11.2 ± 6.8%), τ (21.4 ± 7.6 ms), rheobase (51.9 ± 33.9 pA), firing threshold (−38.2 ± 3.1 mV), amplitude of action potentials (65.1 ± 9.1 mV), rise t...
findings.effect_size: Rin 404.7 ± 212.8 MΩ; sag 11.2 ± 6.8%; τm 21.4 ± 7.6 ms; rheobase 51.9 ± 33.9 pA; AP threshold −38.2 ± 3.1 mV; AP amplitude 65.1 ± 9.1 mV (mean ± SD across layers)
findings.evidence: whole-cell current-clamp from VIP-Cre × Ai14 mouse barrel cortex; layer-by-layer comparisons performed with biocytin reconstructions
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: input resistance (population average of 404.7 ± 212.8 MΩ), sag index (11.2 ± 6.8%), τ (21.4 ± 7.6 ms), rheobase (51.9 ± 33.9 pA), firing threshold (−38.2 ± 3.1 mV), amplitude of action potentials (65.1 ± 9.1 mV)
findings.study_system: mouse VIP-IRES-Cre × Ai14 barrel cortex slices; whole-cell patch

findings.claim: When classified by Petilla criteria, the firing-pattern composition of mouse barrel-cortex VIP+ neurons across all layers is 75.4% continuous-adapting, 14.5% irregular-spiking, 5.8% bursting, and 4.3% high-threshold bursting non-adapting.
findings.claim_source_sentence: 2008), showed the following firing patterns: continuous adapting (75.4%), irregular spiking (14.5%), bursting (5.8%), or high threshold bursting nonadapting (4.3%; see Figs 4 C and 5 C for examples).
findings.effect_size: CA 75.4%, IS 14.5%, B 5.8%, HTBNA 4.3% (whole population)
findings.evidence: current-clamp characterization of VIP-Cre/tdTomato neurons across L2/3–L6 barrel cortex; Petilla nomenclature applied to firing-pattern at rheobase
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: continuous adapting (75.4%), irregular spiking (14.5%), bursting (5.8%), or high threshold bursting nonadapting (4.3%)
findings.study_system: mouse VIP-IRES-Cre × Ai14 barrel cortex; Petilla classification

findings.claim: Bursting VIP+ neurons in mouse barrel cortex are confined to layer II/III; layers IV–VI lack bursting VIP+ cells while still expressing IS (14.7%) and HTBNA (2.9%) variants alongside the dominant CA (82.4%) firing pattern.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In layers IV–VI, 82.4% of all VIP neurons were continuous adapting, 14.7% irregular spiking, and 2.9% high threshold bursting nonadapting.
findings.effect_size: L2/3 — CA 67.6%, IS 14.7%, bursting 11.8%, HTBNA 5.9%; L4–L6 — CA 82.4%, IS 14.7%, HTBNA 2.9%; bursting absent below L3
findings.evidence: VIP-Cre/tdTomato whole-cell, layer-stratified analysis of firing-pattern frequencies; bursting cells observed only in L2/3
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: In layer II/III, 67.6% of all VIP neurons were continuous adapting, 14.7% irregular spiking, 11.8% bursting, and 5.9% high threshold bursting nonadapting.
findings.study_system: mouse VIP-IRES-Cre × Ai14 barrel cortex; layer-by-layer firing-pattern survey

findings.claim: In mouse VISp, the GABAergic taxonomy comprises four classical subclasses (Pvalb, Sst, Vip, Htr3a+/Vip-) plus an additional Sncg subclass that transcriptomically bridges Vip and Lamp5 (renamed from Htr3a+/Vip-).
findings.claim_source_sentence: Our recent single-cell transcriptomic study of two mouse cortical areas (Tasic et al., 2018), VISp and anterolateral motor cortex (ALM), confirmed these four major subclasses and revealed an additional subclass (Sncg) that is related to the Vip and Htr3a+/Vip-(renamed to Lamp5) subclasses.
findings.effect_size: 5 GABAergic subclasses identified (Pvalb, Sst, Vip, Lamp5, Sncg)
findings.evidence: Patch-seq + scRNA-seq integration across VISp and ALM, n=2,955 cells with consistent t-type assignment
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Our recent single-cell transcriptomic study of two mouse cortical areas (Tasic et al., 2018), VISp and anterolateral motor cortex (ALM), confirmed these four major subclasses and revealed an additional subclass (Sncg) that is related to the Vip and Htr3a+/Vip-(renamed to Lamp5) subclasses.
findings.study_system: mouse VISp + ALM Patch-seq; transcriptomic taxonomy

findings.claim: Patch-seq electrophysiological 'subthreshold-2' principal component is highly correlated with input resistance (Pearson r = 0.90), and a separate 'subthreshold (normalized) 1' PC reflects sag amplitude during hyperpolarizing current — providing two orthogonal axes of intrinsic-property variation that can be regressed onto t-type identity.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Many of the sPCs were strongly related to traditional electrophysiological features; for example, the “subthreshold 2” sPC was correlated with input resistance (Pearson’s r = 0.90), and the “subthreshold (normalized) 1” sPC reflected the amount of sag induced by hyperpolarizing current steps (lower ...).
findings.effect_size: sPC2 ↔ Rin: r = 0.90 (Pearson); sPC1(norm) ↔ sag
findings.evidence: PCA over 4,200+ Patch-seq cells; principal-component-vs-feature correlations across all neurons (excitatory + inhibitory).
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: the “subthreshold 2” sPC was correlated with input resistance (Pearson’s r = 0.90)
findings.study_system: mouse VISp Patch-seq

findings.claim: Vip subclass t-types are distributed in a layer-stratified pattern: Vip Col15a1 Pde1a sits at the L1–L2/3 border, Vip Chat Htr1f spans L2/3, and Sncg Vip Itih5 occurs across multiple layers — implying laminar location is partially diagnostic of intrinsic-property variants.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Similarly, some t-types were found right at the L1-L2/3 border (Vip Col15a1 Pde1a, Pvalb Vipr2), while others were distributed across L2/3 (Vip Chat Htr1f, Pvalb Tpbg) (Figure 2B).
findings.effect_size: qualitative laminar map of Vip and Sncg t-types
findings.evidence: Patch-seq + post-hoc soma localization in VISp/ALM
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Similarly, some t-types were found right at the L1-L2/3 border (Vip Col15a1 Pde1a, Pvalb Vipr2), while others were distributed across L2/3 (Vip Chat Htr1f, Pvalb Tpbg) (Figure 2B).
findings.study_system: mouse VISp Patch-seq with laminar mapping

findings.claim: Mouse VISp neurons partition into 17 distinct intrinsic electrophysiological types (e-types) that differ across firing-pattern descriptors (adapting, irregular, transient), AP shape, subthreshold properties (membrane time constant, sag), and f–I curve location/shape.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Specific e-types differed from each other across a variety of characteristics, including firing patterns (e.g., adapting, irregular, transient), AP shape, subthreshold properties (membrane time constant, sag), and f-I curve location and shape.
findings.effect_size: 17 e-types identified
findings.evidence: standardized current-clamp recordings of >1,800 mouse VISp neurons; unsupervised clustering on AP and f–I features
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: We next examined basic electrophysiological characteristics of the 17 identified e-types, such as the AP shape, the estimated membrane capacitance and input resistance, the average f-I curve, and the coefficient of variation of the ISI durations (CV ISI, Fig.
findings.study_system: mouse VISp; standardized intracellular recordings + biocytin reconstruction

findings.claim: Cortical inhibitory neurons exhibit variation along transcriptomic continua that predict their intrinsic physiological properties; a single transcriptomic continuum explains a large portion of differences in state-modulation and intrinsic membrane properties across Pvalb/Sst/Vip/Lamp5/Sncg subtypes.
findings.claim_source_sentence: These differences in state modulation were explained in large part by a single transcriptomic continuum, which also correlated with the intrinsic membrane properties and morphology of these subtypes as assessed in vitro 7, and with their expression of excitatory and inhibitory cholinergic receptors.
findings.effect_size: qualitative — 1-D transcriptomic axis correlates with both physiology and state-modulation
findings.evidence: in vivo two-photon Ca2+ imaging during locomotion + post-hoc transcriptomics in mouse V1; cross-referenced with Patch-seq intrinsic-property atlases
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: These differences in state modulation were explained in large part by a single transcriptomic continuum, which also correlated with the intrinsic membrane properties and morphology of these subtypes as assessed in vitro 7.
findings.study_system: mouse V1 in vivo imaging + scRNA-seq + intrinsic-property reference atlases

findings.claim: During locomotion, the Sncg, Vip, and Lamp5 GABAergic subclasses are on average more active than Pvalb (which dominates oscillation states), demonstrating subclass-specific recruitment patterns that map onto distinct intrinsic-property profiles.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We modified the nested permutation test to compare activity between states, and found significant differences between subclasses (P < 0.001), with the Sncg, Vip and Lamp5 subclasses being on average more active during running and the Pvalb subclass more active during oscillation.
findings.effect_size: P < 0.001 across subclasses, nested-permutation test
findings.evidence: two-photon imaging of state-dependent activity in mouse V1 with post-hoc transcriptomic identification
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: found significant differences between subclasses (P < 0.001), with the Sncg, Vip and Lamp5 subclasses being on average more active during running
findings.study_system: mouse V1, awake behavior + scRNA-seq

findings.claim: Approximately 40% of cortical 5HT3aR interneurons co-express VIP, while the remaining 5HT3aR neurons (mostly Lamp5/Reelin+) are non-VIP — defining the two principal CGE-derived branches with distinct intrinsic-property repertoires.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The 5HT3aR group can be divided in two subgroups based on whether or not a 5HT3aR IN expresses vasointestinal pepetide (Vip), which is found in about 40 % of all 5HT3aR INs and is not expressed in PV or Sst neurons (Lee et al., 2010).
findings.effect_size: VIP+ ≈ 40% of 5HT3aR INs; non-overlapping with PV/Sst
findings.evidence: review consolidating Lee 2010 and downstream classifications across mouse S1/V1
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: VIP, which is found in about 40 % of all 5HT3aR INs and is not expressed in PV or Sst neurons.
findings.study_system: review of mouse cortical 5HT3aR/Vip taxonomy

findings.claim: 5HT3aR-expressing interneurons constitute ~30% of all neocortical INs and form the most heterogeneous of the three principal IN groups (PV, Sst, 5HT3aR); all 5HT3aR INs express functional 5HT3a and nicotinic receptors, conferring strong serotonergic and cholinergic depolarization that gates their firing in vivo.
findings.claim_source_sentence: 5HT3aR INs 5HT3aR INs represent ~30% of all neocortical INs and are thought to be more heterogeneous than the PV and Sst groups.
findings.effect_size: ~30% of cortical INs; universal 5HT3a + nAChR co-expression
findings.evidence: review/compilation across mouse neocortex; Lee 2010 quantification of 5HT3aR fraction
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: However, all 5HT3aR INs express functional 5HT3a and nicotinic receptors (Lee et al., 2010).
findings.study_system: review of mouse cortical interneuron taxonomy

findings.claim: Hippocampal CA1 GAD65-GFP+ interneurons (≈20% of CA1 INs, predominantly reelin+ CGE-derived) cluster into five electrophysiological groups by adaptation, onset delay and ISI regularity, with input resistances ranging from 377 ± 54 MΩ in fast-spiking subset to 468 ± 30 MΩ in delayed-onset adapting subset; VIP+/CR+ cells largely fall into the regular- and irregular-spiking subsets.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Resting membrane potential (mV) −49±1 −51±2 −55±2 −58±2 −51±2 group 4<group 1,2,5 (p = 0.002) Input resistance (MΩ) 468±30 377±54 ...
findings.effect_size: Rin: group 1 (delayed-onset adapting RS) 468 ± 30 MΩ, group 2 (FS) 377 ± 54 MΩ; ~20% of CA1 INs are GAD65-GFP+
findings.evidence: GAD65-GFP CA1 interneurons (P14–21), whole-cell patch + post-hoc immunolabeling for reelin, VIP, CR, CCK; n = 48 cells across 5 firing groups
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Input resistance (MΩ) 468±30 377±54
findings.study_system: mouse GAD65-GFP CA1, juvenile slice, current-clamp + IHC
findings.caveats: Identifies VIP cells as a subset of group 1/3 cells but does not partition cohort by VIP only; intrinsic-property numbers cover the broader GAD65-GFP+ population.

findings.claim: Cortical L2/3 and L5 PV, SST, and VIP interneurons exhibit a complementary connectivity rule: VIP cells preferentially inhibit SST cells (with weak inhibition of PV and other VIP cells), establishing a mutual-inhibition motif whose intrinsic-property differences (high-Rin, irregular/adapting VIP vs low-Rin fast-spiking PV vs accommodating SST) underlie VIP-driven disinhibition.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Pvalb cells preferentially inhibit one another, Sst cells avoid one another and inhibit all other types of interneurons and VIP cells preferentially inhibit Sst cells.
findings.effect_size: VIP→SST inhibition dominant; VIP→PV minimal; PV→PV self-inhibition; SST broadly inhibits PV/VIP/PCs but not SST
findings.evidence: Cre-driven ChR2 photostimulation of each population separately + paired patch in mouse cortex L2/3 and L5; quantification of inhibitory connection probability across all 6 directions
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Pvalb cells preferentially inhibit one another, Sst cells avoid one another and inhibit all other types of interneurons and VIP cells preferentially inhibit Sst cells.
findings.study_system: mouse V1 L2/3 + L5; PV-Cre, Sst-Cre, VIP-Cre x ChR2; paired/triple patch

findings.claim: VIP-Cre cell population is entirely contained within the HTR3A-GFP population (100% overlap of VIP-Cre/tdTomato with HTR3A-GFP) and shows zero overlap with PV-Cre or Sst-Cre populations, validating VIP-Cre as a clean genetic handle that selects a subset of the broader 5HT3aR class.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Virtually all VIP-Cre cells (visualized by tdTomato expression) also expressed green fluorescent protein (GFP) (100%; Fig.
findings.effect_size: VIP-Cre overlap: HTR3A-GFP 100%; PV-Cre 0%; Sst-Cre 0%
findings.evidence: VIP-Cre×Ai14 cross with HTR3A-GFP reporter mice; quantitative co-localization across L2/3 and L5
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Thus, the VIP-Cre population is a sub-population of and is entirely comprised in the HTR3A-GFP population.
findings.study_system: mouse V1, VIP-Cre × Ai14 with HTR3A-GFP, PV-Cre, Sst-Cre crosses

findings.claim: VIP+ and L1 interneurons in mouse V1 are facilitated by ACh at lower agonist doses (depolarization at 100 µM) than SOM neurons (which fire only at 10 mM), and at intermediate ACh (1–100 µM) VIP/L1 receive an inhibitory IPSC barrage while SOM cells do not — defining a dose-dependent inhibitory hierarchy that gates VIP firing recruitment in vivo.
findings.claim_source_sentence: These responses were also concentration specific: ACh at 1-100 μM induced responses in VIP and L1 neurons but minimally in SOM neurons while ACh at 10 mM induced large responses in all 3 cell types (Fig.
findings.effect_size: VIP/L1 fire at 10 mM ACh; depolarize at 100 μM. SOM fires already at 10 μM
findings.evidence: ex vivo whole-cell current clamp of SOM-tdTom, VIP-tdTom, L1 inhibitory neurons; ACh dose-response with NBQX
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: VIP and L1 neurons depolarized at 100 μM ACh but fired only at 10 mM ACh.
findings.study_system: mouse V1, VIP/SOM-tdTom × WT slices

findings.claim: Conditional ErbB4 deletion in VIP-INs causes a long-term ~4-fold increase in spontaneous firing rate of cortical regular-spiking pyramidal neurons that emerges in adolescence, and abolishes locomotion-induced state transitions — implicating intact VIP-IN excitability in cortical state control.
findings.claim_source_sentence: RS cells in mutants exhibited ~4-fold higher spontaneous firing rates than those in controls (Figure 1D–F).
findings.effect_size: ~4× increased PC spontaneous firing rate in mutants; loss of locomotion-evoked spike-rate increase
findings.evidence: VIP-Cre x ErbB4 fl/fl conditional knockout; in vivo silicon probe recordings; comparison with WT VIP-Cre controls during locomotion
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: RS cells in mutants exhibited ~4-fold higher spontaneous firing rates than those in controls.
findings.study_system: mouse V1, VIP-Cre × ErbB4 fl/fl; awake in vivo recordings

findings.claim: Human cortical layer 1 'rosehip' interneurons (a novel non-rodent CGE-derived type expressing CCK and LAMP5 transcriptomic markers but distinct from VIP cells) fire stuttering or irregular-spiking patterns from a resting potential of −61.3 ± 5.8 mV, with subthreshold oscillations tuned to beta–gamma frequencies (3.8–80 Hz).
findings.claim_source_sentence: Anatomically identified RCs responded to long (800 ms) suprathreshold current injections with stuttering or irregular spiking firing patterns 2 when activated from resting membrane potential (-61.34±5.8 mV, Fig.4A).
findings.effect_size: RMP −61.34 ± 5.8 mV (n=10); β/γ-tuned subthreshold oscillations 3.8–80 Hz
findings.evidence: n = 10 rosehip cells whole-cell patched and biocytin-filled in human parietal/frontal/temporal cortex L1; subthreshold FFT analysis
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: membrane oscillations and firing of RCs were tuned to beta and gamma frequencies (Fig.4E-G).
findings.study_system: human neurosurgical cortical slices L1; whole-cell + biocytin
findings.caveats: Rosehip cells map to LAMP5 transcriptomic types (i5 cluster with weak r=0.34 to mouse Smad3) — they are CGE-derived but distinct from VIP. Included for cluster_04 because they sit on the same Sncg/Lamp5/Vip transcriptomic continuum and inform IS/stuttering firing diversity.

findings.claim: Across the multimodal cell census of mouse primary motor cortex (MOp), GABAergic neurons in cortical layers 2–6 segregate into mutually exclusive PVALB, SST and VIP molecular populations, while Pvalb and Sst are not detectable in layer 1 — anchoring the layer-specific intrinsic-property signatures of CGE-derived (VIP, Lamp5, Sncg) versus MGE-derived populations.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In cortical layers 2-6, most GABAergic neurons have mutually exclusive expression of parvalbumin (PVALB), somatostatin (SST) or vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP) 21.
findings.effect_size: Three mutually exclusive subclasses (Pvalb, Sst, Vip) span L2–L6; Pvalb/Sst absent in L1
findings.evidence: Integrated multi-omic atlas: 7 sc/snRNA-seq + snmC-seq2 + snATAC-seq datasets in mouse MOp; cross-species mapping to marmoset and human
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: In contrast, Pvalb and Sst are not expressed in mouse layer 1 by in situ hybridization (ISH).
findings.study_system: mouse + marmoset + human primary motor cortex multimodal atlas

findings.claim: Cortical IS-type VIP+ interneurons that target SST cells (acting as the disinhibitory motif) are intrinsically biased toward firing irregularity by KCNQ/M-current expression and are sensitized to neuromodulatory depolarization by ACh and 5-HT, switching firing patterns in response to brain state.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Furthermore, certain VIP+ cells in barrel cortex are sensitive to depolarization-inducing neuromodulation from acetylcholine or serotonin, which can effectively switch their firing patterns (Prönneke et al., 2019).
findings.effect_size: qualitative neuromodulator-induced firing-pattern switching
findings.evidence: review consolidating Pronneke 2019 (mouse barrel cortex VIP-Cre with ACh + 5-HT bath application) and Goff/Goldberg KCNQ pharmacology
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Furthermore, certain VIP+ cells in barrel cortex are sensitive to depolarization-inducing neuromodulation from acetylcholine or serotonin, which can effectively switch their firing patterns.
findings.study_system: review of mouse barrel cortex VIP-IRES-Cre electrophysiology

findings.claim: The large majority of mouse cortical Vip interneurons display a vertically oriented, bipolar-like dendritic morphology with axons descending into deeper layers — an anatomical signature that is independent of the IS firing-pattern axis (a CA cell can be bipolar; an IS cell can be multipolar).
findings.claim_source_sentence: The large majority of Vip INs have a vertically oriented, bipolar-like dendritic morphology with descending axons into deeper layers.
findings.effect_size: qualitative — bipolar morphology dominant in Vip INs
findings.evidence: review consolidating Pronneke 2015 + Bayraktar 2000 + Cauli 1997 morphological datasets
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: The large majority of Vip INs have a vertically oriented, bipolar-like dendritic morphology with descending axons into deeper layers.
findings.study_system: review of mouse cortical Vip morphology and electrophysiology

findings.claim: Three non-overlapping cortical interneuron populations — PV, SOM and VIP — collectively account for ~85% of neocortical inhibitory neurons, with VIP cells dispensing local disinhibition through a network motif that is partially constrained by their relatively few but specific local pyramidal-cell drivers.
findings.claim_source_sentence: PVs, together with somatostatin expressing cells (SOMs) and vasoactive intestinal peptide expressing cells (VIPs) form three non-overlapping populations that account for ~85% of neocortical INs (Rudy et al., 2011), and have partially dissociable roles in neural computations.
findings.effect_size: PV+SOM+VIP ≈ 85% of cortical INs
findings.evidence: in vivo two-photon Ca2+ imaging in mouse V1 with Cre-driven labeling of PV/SOM/VIP
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: PVs, together with somatostatin expressing cells (SOMs) and vasoactive intestinal peptide expressing cells (VIPs) form three non-overlapping populations that account for ~85% of neocortical INs.
findings.study_system: mouse V1 in vivo + cited literature

findings.claim: PV-, SST- and 5HT3aR-expressing cortical interneurons together account for nearly 100% of GABAergic neurons in mouse somatosensory cortex, with the 5HT3aR group (which contains all VIP+ cells plus VIP-negative L1/Lamp5 cells) forming the third major group whose intrinsic electrophysiology is the most heterogeneous.
findings.claim_source_sentence: This analysis has provided strong evidence that three major groups of neurons, those expressing the Ca 2+ -binding protein parvalbumin (PV), those expressing the neuropeptide somatostatin (SST), and those expressing 5HT3aRs account for nearly 100% of GABAergic neurons in somatose...
findings.effect_size: PV + SST + 5HT3aR ≈ 100% of cortical GABAergic INs (S1)
findings.evidence: review compiling 5HT3aR-BAC EGFP genetic targeting + scRT-PCR/IHC overlap data in mouse S1
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: three major groups of neurons, those expressing the Ca 2+ -binding protein parvalbumin (PV), those expressing the neuropeptide somatostatin (SST), and those expressing 5HT3aRs account for nearly 100% of GABAergic neurons in somatosensory cortex.
findings.study_system: review of mouse cortical interneuron taxonomy

findings.claim: VIP-expressing 5-HT3aR+ cortical interneurons in mouse S1 are electrically the most excitable cortical interneuron group, characterized by very high input resistance, long membrane time constant, prominent sag and low rheobase, with adapting or bursting firing dominating over irregular subtypes; this contrasts sharply with the NPY-cluster (Lamp5/neurogliaform) of 5-HT3aR+ cells.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Cells of the VIP-cluster exhibited the highest input resistance and membrane time constant of our sample.
findings.effect_size: VIP-cluster (n=22): Rin 761.5 ± 306.5 MΩ, τm 40.6 ± 19.5 ms, sag 12.1 ± 7.8%, rheobase 17.7 ± 9.2 pA, RMP −56.0 ± 3.9 mV, 1st-AP duration 0.95 ± 0.37 ms, AP amplitude 83.8 ± 9.8 mV; firing 68% adapting, 32% bursting (n=15/22 vs 7/22). NPY-cluster (n=31): Rin 391.9 ± 124.2 MΩ, τm 25.7 ± 12.1 ms, sag 5.8 ± 6.0%, rheobase 64.8 ± 71.4 pA
findings.evidence: scRT-PCR + whole-cell patch clamp on biocytin-filled 5HT3aR-EGFP+ cortical INs in mouse S1 (Lee 2010 Table 1)
findings.study_system: mouse S1 cortex 5-HT3aR-EGFP transgenic; whole-cell + scRT-PCR

_collapsed_provenance.claim: In juvenile mouse S1, 5-HT3aR-expressing interneurons partition unsupervised into two clusters: a VIP-cluster (n=22) and an NPY-cluster (n=31). The VIP-cluster occupies layers II–III exclusively (no L1 cells), exhibits bipolar vertically oriented dendrites, and has the highest input resistance and longest membrane time constant of the entire sample — establishing the VIP intrinsic-physiology fingerprint as a high-input-resistance bipolar interneuron with two firing modes.

findings.claim: In rat neocortex, 5-HT3 receptors mediate fast serotonergic EPSPs selectively in CCK/VIP-co-expressing GABAergic interneurons, providing a major route for fast neuromodulatory recruitment of VIP cells that contributes to their state-dependent firing on top of M-current/KCNQ-driven irregularity.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The 5-HT3A receptor subunit was expressed selectively in a subset of GABAergic interneurons coexpressing cholecystokinin (CCK) and vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP).
findings.effect_size: 5-HT-induced fast depolarization mediated by 5-HT3A; selective for VIP/CCK INs
findings.evidence: patch-clamp + scRT-PCR + histochemistry in rat acute cortical slices
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: In 5-HT3A-expressing VIP/CCK interneurons, serotonin induced fast membrane potential depolarizations.
findings.study_system: rat neocortex; whole-cell + scRT-PCR

findings.claim: In layers 2–3 of monkey prefrontal cortex, GABAergic interneurons fall into 8 morphological classes (4 newly described) whose intrinsic-electrophysiological properties (firing pattern, AP shape) and molecular markers (CR/CB/Parv) diverge from the rodent canonical taxonomy — establishing that primate VIP/CR-expressing interneurons cannot be assumed to share rodent intrinsic-property profiles.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Consequently, in this study we characterized 8 different morphological types of interneurons from monkey prefrontal cortex, 4 of which have not been previously classified.
findings.effect_size: 8 morphological types; 4 novel; differ from rodent canonical types
findings.evidence: whole-cell patch + biocytin + IHC in monkey dlPFC L2/3
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: we characterized 8 different morphological types of interneurons from monkey prefrontal cortex, 4 of which have not been previously classified.
findings.study_system: macaque dlPFC L2/3; whole-cell + biocytin

findings.claim: Neocortical SST interneurons are intrinsically heterogeneous, comprising Martinotti-type, low-threshold-spiking, irregular-spiking and stuttering subtypes (the latter mostly in L4/L5b) plus some fast-spiking cells; this rich SST diversity provides the comparative substrate for VIP→SST disinhibitory specialization, where VIP cells specifically synapse onto SST neurons and suppress SST firing via GABA-A receptors.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Neocortical SST neurons include Martinotti-type, low- threshold spiking cells or irregular-spiking neurons observed in layer 2/3 (L2/3) and L5a, stuttering non-pyramidal cells in L4 and L5b, and some fast-spiking neurons.
findings.effect_size: SST: ≥4 firing-pattern subtypes (Martinotti/LTS, IS, stuttering, FS); VIP→SST inhibition via GABA-A
findings.evidence: review consolidating Markram 2004, Wang 2004, Ma 2006, Pfeffer 2013 datasets
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Vasoactive intestinal peptide-expressing interneurons form synapses on to SST neurons, and excitation of VIP neurons suppresses SST-cell activity through GABA A R activation.
findings.study_system: review of mouse + rat cortical SST interneurons

findings.claim: Neuron-Derived Neurotrophic Factor (NDNF) is a highly selective marker for cortical layer-1 interneurons that is mutually exclusive with Pvalb, Sst and Vip — establishing NDNF/Lamp5 L1 cells as the principal non-VIP CGE comparator group whose dendrite-targeting connectivity and slow inhibition patterns differ from VIP-mediated disinhibition.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here, we establish Neuron-Derived Neurotrophic Factor (NDNF; Kuang et al., 2010) as a highly selective marker for L1 INs in the adult auditory and prefrontal cortex.
findings.effect_size: NDNF expression Gad2/[Emx1+Pv+Sst+Vip] ≥ 4 — i.e. selectively GABAergic and non-PV/SST/VIP
findings.evidence: RiboTag-seq + Cre-driver lines targeting Pv, Sst, Vip, Gad2 in mouse cortex; ICC validation
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: RiboTag-seq indicates that Ndnf-expression is highly enriched in GABAergic neurons, but not in IN subtypes that express Pv, Sst, or Vip.
findings.study_system: mouse auditory + prefrontal cortex; ribosome-bound RNA-seq, Cre/Flp lines

findings.claim: The Petilla terminology defines the canonical electrophysiological feature axes used to classify cortical GABAergic interneurons — accommodating (continuous adapting), bursting, irregular spiking, fast spiking, low-threshold spiking, stuttering and delayed firing — providing the standardized vocabulary against which VIP-cell firing patterns (CA, IS, BSNAC, HTBNA) are scored.
findings.claim_source_sentence: To tackle this, we convened a representative group of researchers to agree on a set of terms to describe the anatomical, physiological and molecular features of GABAergic interneurons of the cerebral cortex.
findings.effect_size: ≥7 canonical e-feature classes for cortical INs
findings.evidence: consensus expert taxonomy (Petilla Convention)
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: agree on a set of terms to describe the anatomical, physiological and molecular features of GABAergic interneurons.
findings.study_system: expert consensus / nomenclature

findings.claim: Conditional deletion of ErbB4 from VIP+ interneurons during development alters their activity, dysregulates cortical temporal organization and brain-state-dependent firing in adolescence/adulthood, and reduces sensory-evoked cortical responses — implicating NRG1/ErbB4 signaling as a determinant of mature VIP intrinsic-physiology + state modulation.
findings.claim_source_sentence: ErbB4 removal from VIP interneurons during development leads to changes in their activity, along with severe dysregulation of cortical temporal organization and state dependence.
findings.effect_size: qualitative — phenotype emerges in adolescence; reduced sensory responses; impaired sensory learning
findings.evidence: VIP-Cre × ErbB4-flox conditional KO mice; in vivo electrophysiology, behavioral assays
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: These alterations emerge during adolescence, and mature animals in which VIP interneurons lack ErbB4 exhibit reduced cortical responses to sensory stimuli and impaired sensory learning.
findings.study_system: mouse VIP-Cre × ErbB4 flox; in vivo electrophysiology + behavior

findings.claim: VIP-expressing interneurons make up roughly 10% of cortical inhibitory neurons and form a population distinct from the two major Pvalb and Sst groups; their disinhibitory role is exerted by transient suppression of primarily SST and a fraction of PV cells, and their activity is uniformly recruited by reinforcement signals (reward/punishment) across auditory and prefrontal cortex.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Indeed, VIP expression demarcates a small population of all interneurons (~10%), distinct from the two major interneuron populations defined by parvalbumin (Pv) and somatostatin (Som) expression.
findings.effect_size: VIP ≈ 10% of cortical INs; reinforcement signals 'strongly and uniformly' activate VIP
findings.evidence: in vivo single-cell recordings + optogenetic activation in auditory and mPFC of awake mice (Pi et al 2013)
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: we identified a basic disinhibitory circuit module in which activation of VIP interneurons transiently suppresses primarily somatostatin- and a fraction of parvalbumin-expressing inhibitory interneurons.
findings.study_system: mouse auditory cortex + mPFC; in vivo + in vitro optogenetics

findings.claim: Cortical VIP+ interneurons are nonspecifically active across behavioural states (immobility, anaesthesia, visual stimulation) and their Ca2+ activity is highly correlated with periods of increased local network activity; optogenetic silencing of VIP+ cells reduces overall network activity, indicating that VIP+-mediated disinhibition sustains baseline cortical excitability rather than being recruited by a single state.
findings.claim_source_sentence: we show that VIP+ cells are nonspecifically active across behavioral states including immobility, under anesthesia, and during visual stimulation.
findings.effect_size: qualitative — VIP+ silencing reduced overall network activity regardless of brain state
findings.evidence: in vivo two-photon Ca2+ imaging of VIP-Cre × GCaMP in mouse V1 + chemogenetic silencing
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: When we specifically silenced the activity of VIP+ cells, the overall activity in the network was reduced, regardless of brain state of the animal.
findings.study_system: mouse V1 in vivo two-photon imaging; VIP-Cre transgenics

findings.claim: scRNA-seq of mouse VISp and ALM (23,822 cells) defines 61 GABAergic transcriptomic types organized into six subclasses — Sst, Pvalb, Vip, Lamp5, Sncg, Serpinf1 — that are nearly all shared across the two distant cortical areas, with Vip subclass specifically falling on the CGE-derived branch alongside Lamp5/Sncg/Serpinf1 and clearly separated from MGE-derived Pvalb/Sst.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We define six subclasses of GABAergic cells: Sst, Pvalb, Vip, Lamp5, Sncg and Serpinf1, and two distinct types: Sst-Chodl and Meis2-Adamts19.
findings.effect_size: 61 GABAergic types in 6 subclasses; Vip + Lamp5 + Sncg + Serpinf1 share CGE origin
findings.evidence: deep scRNA-seq with iterative bootstrapped clustering of 23,822 cells from VISp and ALM (Tasic et al 2018)
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: The major division among GABAergic types largely corresponds to their developmental origin in the medial ganglionic eminence (Pvalb and Sst subclasses) or caudal ganglionic eminence (Lamp5, Sncg, Serpinfl and Vip subclasses).
findings.study_system: mouse VISp + ALM scRNA-seq

findings.claim: Adult mouse primary visual cortex contains 18 GABAergic transcriptomic cell types organized under three major classes (Vip, Pvalb, Sst); the Vip class is further subdivided into multiple types — including the Vip-Chat type which uniquely expresses choline acetyltransferase and resides in upper cortical layers — and the Vip-Gpc3 type which sits in deeper layers, providing the t-type architecture against which Patch-seq electrophysiology is mapped.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Our analysis identifies 18 transcriptomic cell types belonging to three previously described major classes of GABAergic cells named after the corresponding markers Vip (vasoactive intestinal peptide), Pvalb (parvalbumin), and Sst (somatostatin).
findings.effect_size: 18 GABAergic t-types; Vip class subdivided into multiple t-types incl. Vip-Chat (upper layers) and Vip-Gpc3 (deeper)
findings.evidence: scRNA-seq of 1,679 cells in adult mouse VISp
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: The Vip major type can be divided into several transcriptomic cell types, all of which appear enriched in upper cortical layers, except the Vip-Gpc3 type.
findings.study_system: mouse VISp scRNA-seq

findings.claim: A whole-isocortex + hippocampal-formation atlas of ~1.3 million cells confirms a stable Vip / Lamp5 / Sncg subclass architecture within the CGE neighborhood across mouse cortex and hippocampus (with 4 Lamp5, 5 Sncg, 6 Vip 'supertypes'), establishing that the Vip class boundaries observed in patch-seq studies of VISp/ALM extrapolate to the entire neocortex and hippocampal formation.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In the CGE neighborhood, the Lamp5 (mostly neurogliaform cells), Sncg and Vip subclasses are divided into 4, 5 (one containing Vip cells), and 6 supertypes, respectively.
findings.effect_size: Vip = 6 supertypes across cortex+HPF; Lamp5 = 4; Sncg = 5
findings.evidence: snRNA-seq + scRNA-seq of ~1.3M cells from mouse adult isocortex + HPF (Yao et al 2021)
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Each neighborhood is further divided into 3 subclasses: Lamp5, Sncg and Vip in CGE, and Sst Chodl, Sst and Pvalb in MGE.
findings.study_system: mouse isocortex + hippocampal formation scRNA/snRNA-seq atlas

_collapsed_provenance.claim: Whole-isocortex+HPF taxonomy (1.3M cells across 17 brain regions; Yao 2021 Cell) divides the CGE-derived neighborhood into 3 subclasses — Lamp5 (mostly neurogliaform, 4 supertypes), Sncg (5 supertypes including a Vip+ supertype), and Vip (6 supertypes) — establishing that the VIP class itself is internally fractionable into at least six conserved supertypes spanning isocortex and hippocampal formation.

findings.claim: VIP and SST interneurons in mouse V1 have complementary contrast tuning: VIP neurons respond strongly to low-contrast front-to-back optic flow congruent with self-motion but are suppressed by other directions and high contrast, while SST cells show the opposite preference — VIP-SST mutual antagonism regulates cortical gain to enhance sensitivity to specific weak stimuli without compromising network stability.
findings.claim_source_sentence: VIP and SST neurons have complementary contrast tuning.
findings.effect_size: qualitative — VIP/SST contrast tuning are mirror-image complementary; gain modulation through mutual antagonism
findings.evidence: in vivo two-photon Ca2+ imaging during visual stimulation + locomotion in mouse V1; computational network model
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Network modeling indicates that VIP-SST mutual antagonism regulates the gain of the cortex to achieve sensitivity to specific weak stimuli without compromising network stability.
findings.study_system: mouse V1 in vivo 2-photon imaging during locomotion

findings.claim: VIP→Martinotti and PV→Martinotti synapses have opposite short-term plasticity: PV→MC inputs show frequency-independent depression while VIP→MC inputs facilitate at high frequencies, providing temporally distinct disinhibitory motifs that interact with the irregular/bursting firing patterns of VIP cells.
findings.claim_source_sentence: PV cell input showed frequency-independent depression, whereas VIP cell input facilitated at high frequencies.
findings.effect_size: PV→MC: depression; VIP→MC: facilitation at high frequencies; PV connections also stronger amplitude
findings.evidence: paired patch-clamp + optogenetics in mouse S1 L2/3 (Karnani et al 2016)
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Paired recordings revealed stronger synaptic input onto MC from PV cells than from VIP cells.
findings.study_system: mouse S1 L2/3; paired recording + ChR2 optogenetics

findings.claim: Cortical ChAT-VIP interneurons (a small subset of the VIP class co-expressing choline acetyltransferase and the vesicular ACh transporter SLC18A3) directly *excite* neighboring L1–L3 interneurons and L2/3 + L6 pyramidal cells through fast cholinergic synaptic transmission, in stark contrast to regular VIP cells which disinhibit pyramidal neurons by suppressing SST cells — making ChAT-VIP a functionally distinct VIP sub-class with its own intrinsic-physiology + behavioral role.
findings.claim_source_sentence: ChAT-VIP neurons directly excite neighbouring neurons in several layers through fast synaptic transmission of acetylcholine (ACh) in rodent medial prefrontal cortex (mPFC).
findings.effect_size: qualitative — ChAT-VIP excite (don't disinhibit); fast cholinergic + GABA co-transmission in 10–20% of postsynaptic INs
findings.evidence: in vitro paired patch-clamp + optogenetics in mouse mPFC; behavioral attention assays
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: In contrast to regular VIP interneurons, ChAT-VIP neurons did not disinhibit pyramidal neurons.
findings.study_system: rodent mPFC; ChAT-Cre/VIP-Cre intersectional

findings.claim: scRNA-seq of mouse VISp/ALM identifies VIP among the four most highly-expressed neuropeptide-precursor (NPP) transcripts in cortical neurons (alongside Npy, Sst, Tac2), and reveals that every neuron type expresses multiple NPPs and NP-GPCRs — implying that the VIP class participates in a dense, layered peptidergic neuromodulatory network whose volume transmission complements its fast GABAergic disinhibition output.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Of these genes, Npy, Sst, Vip and Tac2 rank as the top four overall in pFPKM values, while Cck, Penk and Crh also rank in the top ten.
findings.effect_size: VIP among top 4 NPPs by peak FPKM; 18 NPPs + 29 NP-GPCRs detected; Vipr2 expressed in 0.7% of cells (rare receptor)
findings.evidence: scRNA-seq analysis of 22,439 mouse cortical neurons; NPP/NP-GPCR transcript inventory
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Percentages of the sampled neuron population expressing a given NP-GPCR gene (at greater than 1 CPM) range from more than 72% for Adcyap1r1 down to 0.7% for Vipr2.
findings.study_system: mouse VISp + ALM scRNA-seq (Smith et al 2019 elife)

findings.claim: Multimodal patch-seq integration of >4,200 mouse VISp GABAergic interneurons defines 28 morpho-electric-transcriptomic (MET) types whose layer-specific axon innervation patterns are the principal feature distinguishing them, providing a unified VIP/SST/PV/LAMP5/SNCG taxonomy in which transcriptomic identity, intrinsic electrophysiology, and morphology are mutually predictable.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Through multimodal integrated analysis we define 28 met-types that have congruent morphological, electrophysiological, and transcriptomic properties and robust mutual predictability.
findings.effect_size: 28 MET-types; ~78% met-type prediction accuracy from ephys+morph
findings.evidence: patch-seq characterization of 4,270 mouse VISp interneurons (transcriptome+ephys); 517 reconstructed morphologies (Gouwens et al 2020 Cell)
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Prediction of met-types using combined electrophysiological and morphological features did have a higher accuracy (~78%) than t-type prediction.
findings.study_system: mouse VISp; patch-seq

findings.claim: The Vip subclass partitions into 5 MET-types in mouse VISp (Vip-MET-1 through Vip-MET-5) — all resembling bipolar or bitufted cells but with distinct intrinsic-firing fingerprints: Vip-MET-1 fires regular sustained spikes (Npy+, L2/3-L4), Vip-MET-2 fires irregularly (Ptprt+/Rspo1+, L2/3-L4), Vip-MET-4 shows irregular/transient firing with high hyperpolarization-evoked sag (lower L2/3), and Vip-MET-5 (Crispld2 Htr2c / Crispld2 Kcne4 t-types) innervates all layers including L1 with similarly elevated sag.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We identified five Vip met-types ( Figure 7I – L ), all resembling bipolar or bitufted cells.
findings.effect_size: 5 Vip MET-types; Vip-MET-1 regular sustained, Vip-MET-2 irregular, Vip-MET-4/5 elevated sag
findings.evidence: patch-seq + morphology; community detection on probabilistic mapping (Gouwens 2020)
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Vip-MET-1 also typically had regular, sustained firing while Vip-MET-2 firing was usually irregular.
findings.study_system: mouse VISp; patch-seq

findings.claim: VIP-expressing interneurons split into transcriptionally and morphologically distinct sub-populations: classic bipolar VIP+ cells (vertically oriented axons) form one cluster, while multipolar VIP+/CCK+ cells are basket cells transcriptionally closer to other CCK+ baskets lacking VIP, with VIP+/CCK+ baskets enriched in supragranular layers and CCK+/VIP- baskets enriched in L5/6.
findings.claim_source_sentence: CCK+ basket cells that co-express VIP have a small soma and are particularly abundant in supragranular layers of the cortex, whereas CCK+ basket cells lacking VIP are enriched in layers 5 and 6.
findings.effect_size: qualitative laminar enrichment differences
findings.evidence: review of Lim 2018 + He 2016 + Tasic 2017 transcriptomic and morphological studies
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Multipolar VIP+ cells are transcriptionally very different from bipolar interneurons.
findings.study_system: review of mouse cortex VIP/CCK studies

findings.claim: All cortical VIP+ interneurons originate from the caudal ganglionic eminence (CGE), whereas the MGE and adjacent POA generate essentially all SST+ and PV+ interneurons — establishing a hard developmental separation that constrains the VIP class's adult intrinsic-electrophysiology profile and 5HT3aR expression.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The MGE and adjacent POA generate virtually all interneurons in the SST+ and PV+ classes, whereas the CGE produces all VIP+ and CCK+ cortical interneurons along with several other less abundant types.
findings.effect_size: 100% VIP from CGE; ~100% PV/SST from MGE/POA
findings.evidence: review of fate-mapping + lineage-tracing studies
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: The MGE and adjacent POA generate virtually all interneurons in the SST+ and PV+ classes, whereas the CGE produces all VIP+ and CCK+ cortical interneurons.
findings.study_system: review of mouse fate-mapping

findings.claim: Sensory experience drives a VIP-specific transcriptional response — induction of insulin-like growth factor 1 (IGF1) in VIP+ interneurons — which in turn promotes formation of inhibitory synapses *onto* the VIP cells themselves, providing a homeostatic experience-dependent feedback that adjusts disinhibitory drive without altering VIP intrinsic firing.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In contrast, neuronal activity induces a completely different transcriptional response in VIP+ interneurons, promoting the formation of inhibitory synapses on these cells.
findings.effect_size: qualitative — VIP-specific transcriptional response distinct from PV/SST
findings.evidence: Mardinly et al 2016 (cited in Lim review)
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Sensory experience regulates cortical inhibition by inducing IGF1 in VIP neurons.
findings.study_system: mouse visual cortex; activity-dependent gene programs

findings.claim: VIP interneurons in awake auditory cortex and mPFC receive strong, brief excitatory drive from reinforcement signals — both punishment (air-puff) and reward — eliciting abrupt firing-rate increases that occur after punishment delivery, with reward producing slower more sustained responses; this temporal pattern is consistent with VIP+ cells acting as a behavioral-event gate for cortical disinhibition.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Water reward tended to generate somewhat weaker and more sustained firing rate increases (9/10 VIP neurons, half maximum duration 1.2s).
findings.effect_size: 9/10 VIP neurons reward-responsive; punishment-driven response abrupt; ~20% of cortical neurons modulated by VIP optostimulation
findings.evidence: in vivo single-unit + optogenetics in awake mouse ACx and mPFC; Pi 2013 Nature
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Brief light stimulation of this sparse population of VIP-expressing interneurons (1–2% of cortical neurons) resulted in a disproportionately broad effect, generating significant firing rate changes in ~20% of cortical cells (ACx: 130/495, mPFC: 26/155).
findings.study_system: awake mouse ACx + mPFC; Vip-Cre + ChR2 + extracellular recording

findings.claim: Cortical VIP+ interneurons are non-selectively active across all behavioral states (locomotion, immobility, anesthesia, visual stimulation), and their firing correlates most strongly with mean local-population firing of nearby excitatory neurons; silencing VIP+ cells reduces overall network activity in every state, supporting a state-independent disinhibitory amplification function rather than a state-specific switch.
findings.claim_source_sentence: VIP+ activity correlated most clearly with the mean level of population activity of nearby excitatory neurons during all brain states, suggesting VIP+ cells enable high-excitability states in the cortex.
findings.effect_size: VIP silencing reduces population activity across all 4 tested states
findings.evidence: two-photon Ca2+ imaging + DREADD silencing in mouse V1 (Reimann 2016)
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: The pharmacogenetic blockade of VIP+ cell output reduced network activity during locomotion, nonlocomotion, anesthesia, and visual stimulation.
findings.study_system: awake/anesthetized mouse V1; Vip-Cre + DREADD

findings.claim: In mouse barrel cortex, ~13% of GABAergic neurons are VIP-expressing, with ~60% located in L2/3 and ~40% in L4–L6; L2/3 vs. deeper-layer VIP neurons differ significantly in morphology AND multiple intrinsic membrane properties — establishing intra-class laminar heterogeneity within the VIP population at the cellular-physiology level.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In the barrel cortex, approximately 13% of all GABAergic neurons are VIP expressing.
findings.effect_size: 13% of GABAergic; ~60% L2/3, ~40% deeper; L2/3 RMP -66.3±4.8 mV vs deeper more hyperpolarized
findings.evidence: VIPcre/tdTomato + FISH + IHC + whole-cell patch (n=69 cells; Pronneke 2015)
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: VIP neurons from layer II/III were more depolarized in their resting state than those found in deeper layers (somatic resting membrane potential of −66.3 ± 4.8 vs.
findings.study_system: mouse barrel cortex; VIPcre/tdTomato whole-cell patch

findings.claim: VIP interneurons in mouse barrel cortex display markedly heterogeneous firing patterns: in L2/3, 67.6% are continuous-adapting, 14.7% irregular-spiking, 11.8% bursting, and 5.9% high-threshold bursting non-adapting; in L4–L6, 82.4% are continuous-adapting, 14.7% irregular-spiking, and 2.9% high-threshold bursting non-adapting — bursting is essentially restricted to L2/3 VIP cells.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In layer II/III, 67.6% of all VIP neurons were continuous adapting, 14.7% irregular spiking, 11.8% bursting, and 5.9% high threshold bursting nonadapting.
findings.effect_size: L2/3 67.6% adapting / 14.7% IS / 11.8% bursting / 5.9% HTB-NA; L4-6 82.4% adapting / 14.7% IS / 2.9% HTB-NA
findings.evidence: whole-cell patch in mouse barrel cortex (n=69 cells, Pronneke 2015)
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: In layers IV–VI, 82.4% of all VIP neurons were continuous adapting, 14.7% irregular spiking, and 2.9% high threshold bursting nonadapting.
findings.study_system: mouse barrel cortex; whole-cell patch

findings.claim: Across cortical and hippocampal CA1 areas, VIP+ interneurons can be subdivided into 'interneuron-selective' (I-S) types: I-S1 (VIP−/CR+, low input resistance), I-S2 (VIP+/CR−, intermediate), and I-S3 (VIP+/CR−, high input resistance with irregular or regular spiking) — with the high-input-resistance I-S3 sub-type showing prominent intrinsic-firing irregularities and theta-rhythm-locked spiking, suggesting I-S sub-types map onto distinct intrinsic-physiology motifs of the VIP class.
findings.claim_source_sentence: type, interneuron-specific 2 (I-S2) cells, have somata present near resistance with irregular or regular spiking firing pattern the SR and SLM border, and express VIP, but not CR.
findings.effect_size: I-S3 high input resistance + irregular firing; theta-rhythm-locked spike phase
findings.evidence: review of CA1 and isocortical I-S literature (Pronneke + Guet-McCreight 2020)
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: characterization shows that I-S3 cells exhibit a high input resistance with irregular or regular spiking firing pattern.
findings.study_system: review (rodent CA1 + isocortical VIP)

findings.claim: VIP/CR co-expression in rat cortex shows strong laminar bias: 71% of CR+ cells in L2/3 are also VIP+, and 94% of CR+ cells in L5/6 are VIP+, but a substantial fraction of cells express only one marker (43% VIP-only, 29% CR-only) — making any bare VIP-Cre line incomplete and supporting molecular sub-fractionation of the VIP class with respect to disinhibitory I-S vs. non-I-S identities.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In rats, VIP+/CR+ cells show spatial distributions in layers II/III (i.e., 71% of CR+ cells are also VIP+) and layers V/VI (i.e., 94% of CR+ cells.
findings.effect_size: L2/3: 71% CR+ are VIP+; L5/6: 94% CR+ are VIP+; 43% VIP+ only, 29% CR+ only
findings.evidence: rat cortex IHC (Kawaguchi & Kubota 1997, in Pronneke 2020 review)
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: immunoreactive for either VIP (i.e., 43% of VIP+ cells) or CR (i.e., 29% of CR+ cells), but not both.
findings.study_system: rat frontal/parietal cortex; IHC

findings.claim: VIP+ cells in V1 are recruited bidirectionally by behavioral state during head-fixed locomotion: a positively-locomotion-modulated subpopulation and a negatively-modulated subpopulation coexist; in darkness V1 VIP+ and PV+ cells *remain* locomotion-locked, while SOM+ cells become silent — providing a state-conditional VIP-vs-SOM switch.
findings.claim_source_sentence: During head-fixed locomotion, VIP+ cells were divided based on their activity pattern into those that are positively modulated by velocity, and those.
findings.effect_size: Bidirectional locomotion modulation of VIP+; SOM+ silenced in darkness while VIP+/PV+ remain active
findings.evidence: review of in vivo two-photon imaging of V1 (Pronneke 2020 review summarizing Fu 2014 + others)
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: In darkness, VIP+ and PV+ cells remain locomotion-associated, while SOM+ cells become silent.
findings.study_system: review of mouse V1 in vivo imaging

findings.claim: Cortical VIP+ cells receive strong cholinergic neuromodulation: bath application of nicotine induces large depolarizations in auditory and somatosensory VIP+ cells, providing a non-glutamatergic recruitment pathway that biases VIP intrinsic excitability during arousal/attention states independently of network drive.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Similarly, in auditory cortex, VIP+ cells are very sensitive to nicotinic neuromodulation, where bath application of nicotine can induce large depolarizations.
findings.effect_size: qualitative — large nicotine-evoked depolarization in VIP+ cells
